title: Defer thbemacs Vulpea startup work
description:
Implement the `dotfiles-xn6` recommendation to defer org-roam/Vulpea startup work.

Current finding:
- thbemacs currently loads the note database stack during daemon startup because `org` is configured eagerly and dependent packages follow it.
- `vulpea-db-autosync-mode` starts fswatch/db work as part of startup.

Change:
- Keep Org itself available, but defer `org-roam`, `vulpea`, `vulpea-ui`, `vulpea-journal`, and `consult-vulpea` behind commands and/or an idle timer.
- Start `vulpea-db-autosync-mode` after Emacs is responsive or on first note command.
- Ensure note commands have autoload coverage so existing workflows still work.

Rationale:
- emacs-solo moves expensive/private work off the critical startup path with idle timers or optional lazy loading.
- thbemacs should make the daemon/client usable before starting note DB/fswatch work.

Expected impact:
- Faster startup and less daemon boot fragility.
- Database/fswatch cost shifts from startup to idle time or first note use.

Migration risk:
- First note command may pay a one-time load cost.
- Journal/sidebar/search commands need explicit autoloads/commands to avoid broken keybindings.
- Some local workflows may assume Vulpea autosync is running immediately after startup.

Acceptance criteria:
- Daemon startup no longer starts Vulpea/fswatch before Emacs is responsive.
- Existing keybindings/commands for note finding, journal, sidebar, and consult-vulpea still autoload correctly.
- `vulpea-db-autosync-mode` starts reliably on idle or first note use.
- Record before/after startup measurement and first-note-command latency.

title: Remove thbemacs startup package refresh
description:
Implement the top recommendation from `dotfiles-xn6`: remove synchronous package archive refresh from thbemacs startup.

Current finding:
- `thbemacs/init.el` calls `package-refresh-contents` when its archive cache is stale.
- A cold/stale daemon startup measured about `6.72s` Emacs init time / `7.38s` wall clock, dominated by contacting MELPA/GNU/Nongnu.
- A warm startup after refresh measured about `1.93s` Emacs init time / `2.64s` wall clock.

Change:
- Replace the eager refresh path with an explicit command such as `thb/package-refresh-if-stale`, or schedule refresh via an idle timer after Emacs is responsive.
- Preserve a manual repair path for missing packages.
- Avoid network-dependent package work before the daemon/client is usable.

Rationale:
- emacs-solo does not refresh package archives or install packages on the startup critical path.
- Startup should not fail or block because package archives are stale or the network is unavailable.

Expected impact:
- Largest immediate startup win from the research.
- More reliable daemon startup.

Migration risk:
- Missing packages may no longer self-repair before first use unless the explicit refresh/install command is run.
- Need clear messaging for first-run/bootstrap behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Stale package archive metadata does not trigger a synchronous refresh during daemon startup.
- A manual/interactive command exists to refresh stale package archives.
- thbemacs daemon startup still succeeds with existing installed packages.
- Re-run a warm daemon startup measurement and record before/after numbers in the issue or commit message.

title: Fix beads git sync
description: Configure the dotfiles repo so Beads local database files stay private while portable Beads export/config files are synced through git.
close_reason: Configured Beads initialization, git-tracked metadata/export files, hooks, auto-export, and Dolt push.

title: Migrate thbemacs Go to go-ts-mode
description:
Implement the `dotfiles-xn6` recommendation to migrate thbemacs Go support from external `go-mode` to Emacs 31 built-in tree-sitter Go modes.

Current finding:
- thbemacs requires Emacs 31 and already uses tree-sitter for Markdown.
- thbemacs currently installs/uses external `go-mode` with `eglot-ensure`.
- emacs-solo uses `go-ts-mode` / `go-mod-ts-mode` and buffer-local Go setup.

Change:
- Replace the `go-mode` package declaration with built-in `go-ts-mode` and `go-mod-ts-mode` mappings.
- Preserve existing `eglot-ensure` behavior.
- Preserve the current gopls workspace configuration and keep monorepo/vendor-specific overrides in `local.el`.
- Ensure the Go tree-sitter grammar installation path is covered.

Rationale:
- Aligns with the Emacs 31 built-in-first direction.
- Removes one external package.
- Modernizes syntax support and keeps Go consistent with the tree-sitter Markdown direction.

Expected impact:
- Small dependency reduction.
- Better long-term alignment with Emacs core.

Migration risk:
- Indentation variables/hooks differ from `go-mode`.
- `go.mod` handling should be verified.
- Tree-sitter grammars must be available or auto-installed.

Acceptance criteria:
- Opening `.go` files uses `go-ts-mode` and starts Eglot as before.
- Opening `go.mod` uses the appropriate Go module tree-sitter mode.
- Existing gopls directory filter configuration remains effective.
- A representative Go file can be opened without startup/load errors.

title: Remove agent-shell from thbemacs
description: Remove agent-shell references or configuration from thbemacs if present. Verify the Emacs config still loads cleanly after removal.

title: Clarify beads startup guidance
description: Replace AGENTS guidance that says to run bd onboard with bd prime, since onboard is setup-oriented and redundant in an initialized repo.
close_reason: Updated AGENTS guidance to prefer bd prime and reserve bd onboard for setup.

title: Close-read emacs-solo for thbemacs modernization
description: Close-read https://github.com/LionyxML/emacs-solo and compare its Emacs configuration patterns against thbemacs. Suggest actionable changes focused on startup/runtime performance, lazy loading, package selection, built-in replacements, package manager modernization, and reducing/modernizing dependencies. Capture recommendations with rationale, expected impact, and migration risks.
close_reason: Completed emacs-solo comparison report and filed follow-up implementation tasks.

comments.author: tahir
comments.text: Completed close-read and captured recommendations in docs/plans/2026-07-05-thbemacs-emacs-solo-modernization.md. Created follow-up implementation issues: dotfiles-p18, dotfiles-3jf, dotfiles-wll.

comments.author: tahir
comments.text:
Research folded in from `docs/plans/2026-07-05-thbemacs-emacs-solo-modernization.md`.

Source compared: `LionyxML/emacs-solo` at `16bed87` (`2026-07-02`, `feat: tab cycling won't get out of groups anymore`).
Target: `thbemacs/.config/thbemacs`.

Baseline:
- `thbemacs/init.el`: 2555 lines, 42 `use-package` forms.
- `thbemacs/early-init.el` already has solid startup basics: raised GC threshold, temporary `file-name-handler-alist` disable, `package-enable-at-startup nil`, early GUI chrome suppression, native-comp noise suppression.
- Warm daemon startup after archive refresh measured about `1.93s` Emacs init time / `2.64s` wall clock / `61` activated packages.
- Cold/stale archive startup measured about `6.72s` Emacs init time / `7.38s` wall clock, dominated by synchronous package archive refresh.
- `emacs-solo/init.el`: 4103 lines, 51 `use-package` forms, but mostly built-in packages and local `lisp/emacs-solo-*` modules; package count reported as `0` in its splash.

Key emacs-solo patterns worth stealing:
- No package archive refresh or package installation on the startup critical path.
- `early-init.el` sets `gc-cons-threshold` to `most-positive-fixnum`, restores later to `100MB`, restricts VC to Git, avoids initial light flash, and suppresses unused UI before first frame.
- Built-in-first package selection: `icomplete`, `minibuffer`, `completion-preview-mode`, `tab-bar`, `project`, `vc`, `diff-mode`, `ediff`, `eldoc`, `eglot`, `flymake`, `dired`, `wdired`, `org`, tree-sitter modes.
- Local modules replace small external packages where feasible: mode line, gutters, olivetti-like centering, icons, formatter, movement helpers, GitHub/container UIs.
- State/cache files are centralized under cache paths rather than scattered through the config directory.
- Expensive/private code is loaded after startup via idle timers or optional private files.

Prioritized recommendations:
1. Remove synchronous package refresh from startup. Largest startup win; avoids network-dependent daemon failures. Filed as `dotfiles-p18`.
2. Defer org-roam/Vulpea autosync until after startup or first note command. Moves fswatch/db work off the critical path. Filed as `dotfiles-3jf`.
3. Move Go support from external `go-mode` to Emacs 31 `go-ts-mode`/`go-mod-ts-mode`. Removes one dependency and modernizes syntax support. Filed as `dotfiles-wll`.
4. Audit the completion stack against Emacs 31 built-ins. Prototype replacing some of `vertico`, `orderless`, `marginalia`, `corfu`, and `cape` with `icomplete-vertical-mode`, `completion-preview-mode`, built-in `flex`/custom orderless style, and tuned minibuffer variables; keep `consult` initially.
5. Replace `ultra-scroll` with built-in `pixel-scroll-precision-mode` if trackpad behavior is acceptable.
6. Prototype built-in `tab-bar`/`project` workspaces before keeping `tabspaces` permanently.
7. Keep Magit for now, but import low-risk built-in VC/diff improvements from emacs-solo.
8. Centralize cache/state paths with a small `thb/cache-path` helper.
9. Lazy-load non-critical `local.el`/private overrides with an idle timer where safe.

Suggested implementation order: package refresh, Go tree-sitter migration, Vulpea deferral, built-in scroll trial, cache path cleanup, completion prototype, tabspaces prototype, VC/Magit-light exploration.
